old_exact: either: B A) B) acknowledges facts exist that support the guilty plea; or while maintaining innocence, acknowledges that the guilty plea is knowingly, voluntarily and intelligently made by the defendant and that evidence exists from which the trier of fact could reasonably conclude that the defendant committed the crime.
new_exact:
either:
       A)     acknowledges facts exist that support the guilty plea; or
       B)     while maintaining innocence, acknowledges that the guilty plea
              is knowingly, voluntarily and intelligently made by the
              defendant and that evidence exists from which the trier of fact
              could reasonably conclude that the defendant committed the
              crime.
source_quote:
[¶10] After determining there is a factual basis for the plea, the district court must
also find that the defendant either:
       A)     acknowledges facts exist that support the guilty plea; or
       B)     while maintaining innocence, acknowledges that the guilty plea
              is knowingly, voluntarily and intelligently made by the
              defendant and that evidence exists from which the trier of fact
              could reasonably conclude that the defendant committed the
              crime.
N.D.R.Crim.P. 11(b)(4).
note: At [¶10], the DB has corrupted a formatted list structure. The source PDF shows A) and B) as properly formatted separate list items with their own lines and indentation. The DB has incorrectly collapsed this into 'either: B A) B) acknowledges...' as a single line of text. This is a clear ingestion/formatting error where list markers were lost during text extraction and processing. The word count difference (17 words) corresponds to the extra whitespace/newlines needed to properly format the list items.

source_quote:
II
[¶3]   "When a court has accepted a plea and imposed sentence, the defendant cannot
withdraw the plea unless withdrawal is necessary to correct a manifest injustice."
State v. Bates, 2007 ND 15, ¶ 6, 726 N.W.2d 595.
note: The '*101' appears on line 19 of the DB as a stray artifact from OCR/ingestion processing. The PDF shows this text flows directly from 'withdrawal' to 'is necessary' with no page marker in the opinion body itself. The page number '1' in the PDF is a footer/header element, not part of the opinion text.

new_exact: meaning is given by useage. N.D.C.C. § 9-07-09.
source_quote: meaning is given by useage. N.D.C.C. § 9-07-09. If the parties' intent can be
evidence: DB shows 'usage' (normalized/corrected spelling); authoritative PDF source shows 'useage' (archaic or printed form). This is in the opinion's recitation of N.D.C.C. § 9-07-09. The source byte-exact substring verified in pdftotext output.
note: The word 'useage' appears in the court's printed opinion and is likely either (1) the actual spelling in the statute N.D.C.C. § 9-07-09, or (2) a typo in the opinion/statute. Per fidelity requirement, the DB should match the court's published text.

source_quote: Code. "An order which grants or refuses a new trial or which sustains a demurrer"
evidence: The source PDF (extracted via pdftotext) shows the text flows directly from 'grants or' to 'refuses' with no embedded page number. The DB contains a stray asterisked page marker ' *83' that does not appear in the authoritative source. This is an ingestion/OCR artifact—a corrupted page number marker embedded in the text.
note: The ' *83' marker is a remnant from PDF processing. Legal opinions use inline pinpoint citations (e.g., '479 N.W.2d 863, 83') but NOT stray asterisked markers on separate lines. Removing this restores the intended text flow.

source_quote: this is the "infrequent harsh case" warranting certification
evidence: The source PDF shows the phrase 'infrequent harsh case' as a continuous phrase with no embedded page number. The DB contains ' *84' between 'infrequent' and 'harsh', which is not present in the authoritative source. This is another ingestion/OCR artifact—a corrupted page marker.
note: Similar to the *83 marker above, this is a stray asterisked page number that disrupts the text flow. The source shows clean continuous text without this marker.

source_quote: You are also instructed that the Plaintiff had a duty under the law to "mitigate" her damages—that is, to exercise reasonable diligence

This is a typographic dash used in legal writing to separate related clauses, not a hyphen, and represents an OCR/extraction defect in the database.

note: The '*431' text marker represents page 431 of the official North Western 2d reporter (925 N.W.2d). Per Rule 8, page breaks and page numbering are handled by a separate pass. This marker should not appear within sentence text and breaks the continuity of 'illegal narcotics' into a broken phrase across lines with unnecessary whitespace.
